title: pickeringia montana subsp. tomentosa
intro: Pickeringia montana subsp. tomentosa is a subspecies of the Pickeringia montana plant, commonly known as mountain tickseed, a flowering plant native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, which are attributed to its high concentration of alkaloids and saponins. The health benefits of Pickeringia montana subsp. tomentosa include its potential to reduce inflammation, soothe digestive issues, and exhibit antimicrobial activity, making it a useful remedy for various health concerns. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are often used to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
